As temperatures drop in Lake St. Louis, protecting your home's plumbing becomes crucial. Our expert guide will help you prevent frozen pipes and avoid costly plumbing repairs this winter season.

Why Frozen Pipes Are a Major Concern in Lake St. Louis

Lake St. Louis residents, particularly those in areas like The Moorings and Easternshore Estates, face significant risks when temperatures plummet. Frozen pipes can burst, causing thousands in water damage and requiring emergency repairs.

Essential Steps to Prevent Frozen Pipes

1. Insulate Vulnerable Pipes

  • Identify pipes in unheated areas (crawl spaces, attics, garages)
  • Use foam pipe insulation or heat tape
  • Pay special attention to exterior walls

2. Maintain Consistent Indoor Temperature

  • Keep thermostat at 55°F minimum, even when away
  • Open cabinet doors under sinks
  • Allow warm air to circulate around plumbing

3. Let Faucets Drip

During extreme cold, allow faucets to drip slowly. This prevents pressure buildup and reduces freezing risk. If you notice slow drains, consider scheduling professional drain cleaning.

Warning Signs of Frozen Pipes

  • No water flow from faucets
  • Frost on visible pipes
  • Strange odors from drains
  • Unusual sounds in plumbing system
